body_txt: A Bootstrapped Leitmotif is born when a piece of Background Music becomes tied to a character, place, or other specific part of the story that it was not originally intended to represent. This is often because the song in question is played during scenes that focus heavily on a specific character. Sometimes a character may already have a Leitmotif , but a different song that is more readily identified by the audience usurps it and becomes more widely accepted and recognized. In video games, this commonly occurs with music for stages or levels strongly associated with a certain character, or the character's Boss Battle music. This can also occur if a character comes from a game where characters don't get Leitmotifs but when they become a Guest Fighter in another game that does, they are given a music piece(s) from their home series to serve as their Leitmotif usually in the form of the main theme of their home series or the other music tracks heavily associated with that character. A Sister Trope of Bootstrapped Theme , where a leitmotif or other piece of music comes to stand for the series as a whole.
